stem: A agriculture study measures crop yield across 3 regions, each containing multiple farms. region A has 15 observations with mean 48.9 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.3). region B has 5 observations with mean 55.5 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 3.1). region C has 5 observations with mean 66.0 tonnes/hectare (within-region SD = 9.8). The between-region standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 7.7 and 19.4 tonnes/hectare. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true crop yield for region B, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-region variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for region B: 55.5 tonnes/hectare (shrunk from raw 55.5). Range [55.4, 55.5] as between-region SD varies from 7.7 to 19.4. Pooled mean: 53.6.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 53.6 tonnes/hectare (weighted average of 3 region means).
Shrinkage factor for region B: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/5). At σ_between=12.9: B = 0.989.
Interval [55.4, 55.5] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (55.5) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (53.6) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
